CONTENT.Club career: After playing for the youth teams of Croatian club Rijeka, Smolčić made his professional debut for the club's first team on 17 March 2019, when he appeared as a starter in a 3–1 victory against Osijek. In the 2019–20 season, he played 24 league matches and won the Croatian Cup with his team, appearing in three cup matches, but missing the final against Lokomotiva Zagreb due to being suspended after receiving two yellow cards in the semi-final.On 31 May 2022, it was announced that Smolčić will move to Bundesliga side Eintracht Frankfurt for the 2022–23 season, on a five-year deal until 2027.On 24 June 2024, Smolčić was loaned by LASK in Austria.
CONTENT.Personal life: Hrvoje's twin brother Ivan is also a professional footballer, currently playing with HNK Rijeka. The twins moved to HNK Rijeka Academy together in 2014.
CONTENT.External links: Hrvoje Smolčić at Soccerway.comHrvoje Smolčić at WorldFootball.netHrvoje Smolčić at Soccerbase.comHrvoje Smolčić at FBref.comHrvoje Smolčić at the Croatian Football Federation
CONTENT.International career: On 14 August 2018, Smolčić played his first international game for the Croatian under-19 team, coming off the bench for Leon Kreković in a friendly match against Italy. After playing four more matches for the under-19 team and appearing in two matches for the under-20 squad, he debuted for the under-21 team on 8 October 2021 in a European Under-21 Championship qualifying match against Norway.

SUMMARY: Hrvoje Smolčić (born 17 August 2000) is a Croatian professional footballer who plays as a centre back for Austrian club LASK on loan from the German club Eintracht Frankfurt.
